ai_strategy.summary: Louisiana State University's public AI posture combines published guidance, teaching resources, research centers, AI-relevant programs, and research computing. Public evidence points to distributed governance rather than a verified standalone chief AI officer.

key_provisions: Use appropriate or approved AI tools within institutional data rules
Faculty should set clear course-level AI expectations
Users remain responsible for accuracy, attribution, privacy, and academic integrity
Sensitive data should not be entered into unapproved public tools
ai_policy.summary: LSU publishes faculty and IT guidance for AI use, emphasizing course-level expectations, data protection, and appropriate use of generative AI.

bodies.name: AI technology and teaching guidance owners
bodies.scope: Distributed responsibility for AI tool guidance, teaching support, and research coordination.

bodies.name: LSU artificial intelligence research in Computer Science

governance_structure.summary: Louisiana State University's AI governance is distributed across IT, teaching support, and research units.

initiatives.title: Louisiana State University AI guidance and acceptable-use resources
initiatives.description: LSU publishes faculty and IT guidance for AI use, emphasizing course-level expectations, data protection, and appropriate use of generative AI.
initiatives.significance: Institution-level guidance is the baseline signal for responsible AI adoption.

investment.description: Policy and guidance work; no dollar amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: AI use is permitted within privacy, security, course, and attribution guardrails.

initiatives.title: Louisiana State University generative AI teaching resources
initiatives.description: Louisiana State University publishes faculty-facing resources for syllabus language, assignments, assessment, and student expectations around generative AI.
initiatives.significance: Teaching guidance moves AI adoption from conduct enforcement into course design.

investment.description: Faculty support resources; no amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty set course-level rules and students need clear disclosure and permitted-use expectations.

initiatives.title: LSU artificial intelligence research in Computer Science
initiatives.description: Louisiana State University has AI, machine learning, data science, policy, health, or autonomy research structures that organize faculty and graduate research.
initiatives.significance: Dedicated research structures are a core marker of R1 AI maturity.

investment.description: Research center/institute support; aggregate AI-specific amount not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Cross-school or interdisciplinary AI research ecosystem.

initiatives.title: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Certificate
initiatives.description: Louisiana State University offers AI-relevant degrees, certificates, bootcamps, or structured learning pathways in AI, machine learning, data science, analytics, or computing.
initiatives.significance: AI credentials connect R1 research capacity to student and employer demand.

initiatives.title: Louisiana State University research computing for AI workloads
initiatives.description: LSU HPC provides advanced computing resources for researchers, including computational workloads used in AI and data science.
initiatives.significance: AI maturity depends on scalable compute as well as policy and curricula.

investment.description: Research computing infrastructure; AI-specific public amount not disclosed.

initiatives.title: Louisiana State University responsible and equitable AI access
initiatives.description: Louisiana State University's public AI resources stress transparent expectations, responsible use, and access-aware teaching or public-interest AI work.
initiatives.significance: Equity evidence distinguishes mature AI adoption from simple tool availability.

investment.description: Access and responsible-use work; no amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Clear AI expectations reduce unequal access, ambiguous enforcement, and privacy risk.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: MikeGPT university AI assistant
initiatives.description: LSU launched MikeGPT as an AI tool designed to support the university community with LSU-specific information and needs.
initiatives.significance: This adds a named campus AI assistant rather than only general generative-AI guidance.

computing_infrastructure.summary: LSU HPC documents SuperMike-III as a 1.3 PFLOP cluster with 183 nodes, including 8 GPU nodes with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs each (32 A100 GPUs). Deep Bayou adds a 13-node NSF-funded GPU cluster for deep learning and AI workloads with 11 nodes carrying 2 NVIDIA V100S GPUs and 2 nodes carrying 4 V100S GPUs (30 V100S GPUs), for 62 disclosed GPUs across the two named AI-relevant systems.

investments_and_gifts.purpose: AI research, education, or infrastructure capacity.

investments_and_gifts.title: Law Prof’s AI Sandbox seed grant
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Grant support for LSU Law professors to build an AI Sandbox resource for legal educators exploring classroom AI use and policies.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Interdisciplinary AI research and education coordination.

student_facing_ai.name: Course-level AI expectations
student_facing_ai.scope: Students follow instructor rules and institutional guidance for AI use.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and transparent AI use
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Public guidance emphasizes clarity, responsible use, and access-aware teaching or research practices.

regulatory_context.summary: Louisiana State University operates under federal education, privacy, and research obligations plus campus rules. This sweep found institutional AI guidance but no separate campus-specific AI statute.

narrative_summary: Louisiana State University now has stronger public evidence for named student/community AI tooling and AI compute. MikeGPT is a campus AI assistant designed around LSU-specific information and needs. LSU HPC documents SuperMike-III with 32 NVIDIA A100 GPUs and Deep Bayou with 30 NVIDIA V100S GPUs, creating a source-backed 62-GPU AI/deep-learning compute signal. Teaching evidence also includes the LSU Law Prof’s AI Sandbox, supported by a $5,000 Association of Legal Writing Directors grant. Public gaps remain for enterprise user counts, MikeGPT usage telemetry, comprehensive AI budget totals, and subgroup outcome metrics.

gaps_acknowledged: Current president, provost, CIO, and chief AI officer were not independently refreshed in this worker sweep.
MikeGPT is publicly documented, but user counts, usage telemetry, and platform operating cost were not disclosed.
AI-specific total budget and student subgroup outcome metrics were not disclosed beyond the cited $5,000 law-teaching grant and HPC hardware specifications.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
